title: Set-Based Lookup Optimization
impact: 10-50x speedup on bulk delete operations by replacing O(n) includes() with O(1) Set.has()

tasks.description: Optimize deleteEntities with Set - Replace entityNames.includes() with namesToDelete.has() in EntityManager.deleteEntities() for O(1) lookups instead of O(n)
testCategories: All existing deleteEntities tests must pass
stepByStep: Add 'const namesToDelete = new Set(entityNames);' after getting the graph
acceptanceCriteria: Set created from entityNames array before filtering
All includes() calls replaced with has() calls
No changes to method signature or return type

tasks.description: Run Full Test Suite - Ensure the optimization doesn't break any existing functionality across the entire codebase
acceptanceCriteria: npm run typecheck passes with no errors

tasks.description: Update Benchmark Metrics - Run benchmarks and add Sprint 2 post-optimization metrics to the baseline document with improvement calculations

implementation.analysis: Explain O(n×m) to O(n+m) reduction with concrete numbers

successCriteria: deleteEntities uses Set.has() instead of includes()
Benchmark shows measurable improvement for large deletions
Metrics document updated with Sprint 2 results
notes: This is the highest-impact single change in Phase 6. The Set-based optimization is a classic algorithm improvement. Can run in parallel with Sprint 3 after Sprint 1 completes. The optimization is transparent - same behavior, just faster.
